2605.12156.title: Latent Causal Void: Explicit Missing-Context Reconstruction for Misinformation Detection
2605.12156.paper_summary: Automatic misinformation detection performs well when deception is visible in what an article explicitly states. However, some misinformation articles remain locally coherent and only become misleading once compared with contemporaneous reports that supply background facts the article omits. We study this omission-relevant setting and observe that current omission-aware approaches typically either attach retrieved context as auxiliary evidence or infer a categorical omission signal, leaving the specific missing fact implicit. We propose \emph{Latent Causal Void} (LCV), a retrieval-guided detector that explicitly reconstructs the missing fact for each target sentence and uses it as a textual cross-source relation in graph reasoning. Concretely, LCV retrieves temporally aligned context articles, asks a frozen instruction-tuned large language model to generate a short missing-context description for each sentence--article pair, and feeds the resulting relation text into a heterograph over target sentences and context articles. On the bilingual benchmark of Sheng et al., LCV improves over the strongest omission-aware baseline by $2.56$ and $2.84$ macro-F1 points on the English and Chinese splits, respectively. The results indicate that modeling the missing cross-source fact itself, rather than only attaching retrieved evidence or predicting an omission signal, is a useful representation for omission-aware misinformation detection.
2605.12156.paper_summary_zh: 自動誤資訊檢測在欺騙明顯的情況下表現良好，特別是當文章明確陳述時。然而，一些誤資訊文章在局部上仍然是連貫的，只有在與提供文章省略的背景事實的當前報導進行比較時才會變得誤導。我們研究這種與省略相關的情境，觀察到目前的省略感知方法通常要麼將檢索到的背景作為輔助證據附加，要麼推斷出一個類別省略信號，讓具體缺失的事實保持隱含。我們提出了\emph{潛在因果空缺}（LCV），這是一種檢索引導的檢測器，明確重建每個目標句子的缺失事實，並將其用作圖推理中的文本跨源關係。具體而言，LCV檢索時間對齊的背景文章，請求一個冷凍的指令調整大型語言模型為每個句子—文章對生成一個簡短的缺失背景描述，並將生成的關係文本輸入到目標句子和背景文章的異構圖中。在Sheng等人的雙語基準上，LCV在英語和中文分割上分別提高了$2.56$和$2.84$的宏F1分數，超過了最強的省略感知基線。結果表明，對缺失的跨源事實本身進行建模，而不僅僅是附加檢索到的證據或預測省略信號，對於省略感知的誤資訊檢測是一種有用的表徵。
2605.12156.author: Hui Li et.al.
2605.12156.authors: Hui Li, Zhongquan Jian, Jinsong Su, Junfeng Yao
